---
title: "Consultoria Ratones"
author: "Andres Sanchez"
date: "2023-11-05"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(readxl)
setwd("C:/Users/ANDRES/Trabajos Universidad/Estadistica/Consultoria e IA/Consultoria")
data<-read_excel("Resultados_experimento.xlsx")
```

## Including Plots

```{r, echo=FALSE}
data[1:5]<- lapply(data[1:5] , as.factor)
str(data)
names(data)
```


```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
library(FactoClass)
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)
library(cluster)
```


```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
Y <-data[,6:9]
acp <- PCA(Y, graph = T)
```


```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
fviz_pca_ind(acp, col.ind = data$Tratamiento, palette = 1:6, repel = F)
```

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
fviz_pca_ind(acp, col.ind = data$Bolque, palette = 1:6, repel = F)
```

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
summary(acp)
xtable(acp)
```
